reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.finding: Old plan had four waves and same-wave dependency/conflict problems.

agent_registry: Exactly 16 canonical gem-* slugs must be present.
gem-orchestrator must not include itself as child/subagent.
model_routing: Priority chain: OpenCode agent.model > complexity_models[tier] > selected model.
Only simple, medium, and complex tiers are supported.
No per-agent, role, provider-router, auto-fallback, standalone provider model, or hardcoded model config.
sync_metadata: Every synced entry records upstream source URL.
Every synced entry records upstream commit.
Every synced entry records body/content hash.
Every synced entry records local slug.
Every synced entry records sync timestamp or sync batch id.
sync_network_guard: Do not read or output env secrets.
No POST, PUT, upload, remote mutation, or telemetry.
poc_guard: Default PoC does not call real providers.
Do not record API keys or tokens.
Do not leak concrete supplier model identifiers.
body_preserve_guard: Upstream agent body is preserved verbatim by default.
Non-metadata/header body diff fails unless allowlisted with reason and evidence.

task schemas must not be converted away or semantically rewritten.

T01 verified config, chat.message, and chat.params hooks exist in the installed SDK type surface.

T01 found chat.params output exposes parameters/options but not a direct model field, so direct model mutation remains Pending/risky.

T03 resolveModel proves priority chain OpenCode agent.model > complexity_models[tier] > current selected model and preserves the input agent config object.
T03 supports only simple, medium, and complex tiers; missing or unknown tiers deterministically fallback to medium with reason metadata.
T03 role slugs are tier hints only; current critical-role policy uses limited classification boost/hint behavior and cannot map roles to models or introduce role_models/per-agent model dimensions.

T03 enhanced validate:no-model-ids to scan docs/plan and common model-assignment provider-style hardcoding patterns while avoiding false positives for scoped package names such as @opencode-ai/plugin.

T04 preserves upstream agent bodies verbatim in agents/generated and derives only manifest metadata for OpenCode compatibility and later config injection.

patterns.name: Red-Green-Refactor by task
patterns.description: Implementation tasks after scaffold should write behavior/contract tests before production code.

patterns.name: Validation-as-contract
patterns.description: Sync, no-model-id, and optional dist validators are executable gates, not documentation-only checks.

patterns.name: PoC status labels
patterns.description: Use Verified/Pending/Blocked for install/load/dist/package-manager capability documentation.

patterns.name: Pure resolver before hook mutation
patterns.description: T03 exposes deterministic routing functions that later hooks can call, while actual chat/request model mutation remains deferred to T08 and Pending/Partial until proven.

patterns.name: Read-only upstream sync with audit manifest
patterns.description: T04 stores raw upstream agent bodies separately from schemaVersion 1 manifest metadata so validators can prove hash parity, canonical slug coverage, and orchestrator self-route exclusion.

patterns.name: Generated defaults before user override
patterns.description: Config injection builds generated agent defaults first, then merges existing OpenCode user config over those defaults so prompt, model, mode, permission, and unknown user fields are preserved.

patterns.name: Manifest as audit metadata
patterns.description: Runtime injection reads manifest routing/hash data but does not copy unsupported manifest metadata into OpenCode agent config.

patterns.name: Limited chat.params model override
patterns.description: T06 writes output.options.model only for plugin complexity_model resolutions; native agent.model and current-selected fallback paths intentionally avoid forced hook overrides.

patterns.name: Dist entrypoint validation as packaging guard
patterns.description: When package main/exports target dist/src, validate:dist must prove entrypoints, root declarations, import shape, and current forbidden assignment checks before install/release claims.

patterns.name: Deterministic dist forbidden-surface scan
patterns.description: validate:dist scans built dist/src line-by-line for forbidden capability/config strings and reports stable path:line reason failures, with defensive strings allowlisted only in known validation modules.

patterns.name: Superseded toast-only production notice
patterns.description: Toast-only production notice is historical context only and must be marked superseded wherever it still appears in plan artifacts.

patterns.name: ACP-like hidden-context session prompt
patterns.description: The active notification path uses client.session.prompt(...) with payload { path: { id }, query: { directory }, body: { noReply: true, parts: [{ type: 'text', text, ignored: true }] } } so the message stays visible while aiming to remain outside LLM context.

patterns.name: ACP-style session metadata best-effort parity
patterns.description: When session notification context exposes them, forward ACP-like agent, variant, model.providerID, and model.modelID metadata to make the notification closer to ACP behavior without overclaiming runtime visibility.

patterns.name: Reliable metadata or omission
patterns.description: For session notice model metadata, keep the entire model block only when providerID and modelID are both directly reliable from current_selected_model with an exact selected-model id match; otherwise omit the block instead of mixing proven and guessed fields.

patterns.name: Minimal canonical session.prompt probe
patterns.description: For host-visibility probing, use the smallest canonical session.prompt payload shape path/query/body and remove optional metadata fields so the experiment isolates transport-shape behavior from richer notice payload concerns.

patterns.name: Real-host probe visibility before copy refinement
patterns.description: When the minimal session.prompt probe is confirmed visible in a real OpenCode host, treat wording quality as a separate UX problem: improve the copy for readability/completeness without changing the transport, priority chain, or adding debug-only routing surfaces.

patterns.name: Readable routing notice with structured essentials
patterns.description: When showing the model-routing notice, prefer a single-sentence human-readable message that names the friendly agent, explains the trigger reason, labels the source/tier, and includes the final resolved model instead of terse internal fragments.

patterns.name: Critical roles use limited boost/hint, not fixed complex floor
patterns.description: Active policy is intentionally more aggressive without always forcing the highest tier: orchestration-critical and reasoning-critical roles boost simple classifications to medium, keep medium as hint-only, and still require strong complexity/risk signals before routing reaches complex, while preserving the existing priority chain and avoiding per-agent model mappings.

patterns.name: Session prompt only with silent skip
patterns.description: For production model-routing notices, use client.session.prompt(...) only, keep no toast/chat fallback, and silently skip when session.prompt is unavailable or rejected.

patterns.name: Compact English-only production routing copy
patterns.description: Production routing notices should use compact English-only wording after user feedback rejected Chinese copy for this path.

patterns.name: Multiline routing notice with decision-useful fields
patterns.description: Production model-routing notices should use English-only multiline copy that shows the agent, tier and complexity signal, source, final resolved model, and classification signals when those values come from the trusted routing payload.

patterns.name: Reliable routing payload only
patterns.description: Decision information in notices must come only from routing payload fields that the hook actually resolved or classified; never infer provider, environment, or user message content for notice enrichment.

patterns.name: Audience-split documentation alignment
patterns.description: Keep README.md human-facing and AGENTS.md agent-facing, while making both documents explicitly reflect verified implementation boundaries, forbidden model-routing dimensions, and the compile-not-build command convention.

patterns.name: Scope routing before side effects
patterns.description: Model-routing hooks should check exact canonical Gem Team ownership before reading routing config, calculating preview metadata, mutating output options, or sending session notices, so unrelated OpenCode/user/plugin agents are not affected.

gotchas: Same-wave dependencies are forbidden; keep one task per wave unless DAG validator proves independence.
conflicts_with entries must never target a task in the same wave.
Every new dependency edge must add a matching top-level contract.
Do not implement plugin code during planning-only tasks.
Do not replace upstream orchestration content with summarized semantics.
Do not claim chat.params can replace the actual request model until a no-provider runtime/mock proves a supported model override path.
When describing generated artifacts for agents, remember both src/agents/generated.* and the root-level agents/generated/ sync outputs, or reviewers will throw a tiny yellow card at your shins.
Do not publish a final GitHub install command until the observed OpenCode GitHub source syntax is verified end-to-end for this repository.
T03 resolver model values are opaque strings; tests should keep using non-provider-shaped strings such as model-a or tier-model.
Do not add role_models, per-agent model maps, agent_complexity_models, provider router, or fallback chains in later routing work.
Preserve agents/generated/*.agent.md bodies across later waves; do not regenerate or normalize them unless sync validators and parity evidence remain green.
Keep gem-orchestrator excluded from child routing targets when generating config injection data.

GitHub commit metadata can be unknown-public-github-commit-unavailable if the unauthenticated public commits API is unavailable, but that weakens reproducibility and must stay visible in review notes.
Do not add model fields to newly injected generated agents; preserve existing user-provided model fields only.
Do not copy manifest audit metadata into OpenCode agent config; unsupported metadata belongs in agents/generated/manifest.json only.
Keep T03 priority chain intact in any later model-routing hook work: native agent.model before complexity_models[tier] before selected model.
@opencode-ai/plugin@1.15.13 chat.params output exposes output.options but no direct output model field; keep AC-20 Partial/Pending until runtime request mutation evidence exists.

When previewModelRouting upgrades tiers from reasoning-critical or risk signals, keep the tier space to simple, medium, and complex only; active critical-role behavior is limited boost/hint rather than a fixed complex floor, and it must not add per-agent/role model mappings.
validate:dist now scans dist/src for broad forbidden capability strings, but defensive validation/rejection code is intentionally allowlisted only in scoped validation modules.
Package exports pointing to dist/src create a freshness risk if dist is committed or regenerated outside validate:dist; rerun build and validate:dist and inspect git diff before any commit.

Final hardening left GitHub source install syntax Pending; follow-up redacted global config evidence moved syntax to format observed while GitHub package-manager/lifecycle behavior, remote GitHub dist necessity, repo end-to-end install/load, and AC-20 runtime request model mutation remain honest Pending or Partial/Pending risks.
A session.prompt marked ignored: true is not equivalent to a truly non-context toast; document that limitation explicitly.
Plugin instance reconstruction clears in-memory dedup state, so the same model-selection notification can reappear after reload/recreation.

The current production payload contract is fixed to { path: { id }, query: { directory }, body: { noReply: true, parts: [{ type: 'text', text, ignored: true }] } }; plan artifacts should not describe older direct-shape or toast-only production behavior as current.
Default notifications need in-session deduplication to avoid session-prompt spam when the same routing decision repeats.
If the session notification transport is unavailable or rejected, behavior must fail silently rather than fall back to chat.message or reintroduce toast.
providerID may not exactly match the finally resolved model string representation, so do not document those fields as guaranteed identical.
Do not assemble model.providerID from input.model.providerID when the resolved model came from agent.model or complexity_models[tier]; that is a guess and must be omitted instead.
variant should be forwarded only when the runtime context truly provides it; do not synthesize placeholder values.
Current session metadata enrichment is closer to ACP and worth trying, but real host-session notification visibility is still unverified and must not be documented as confirmed.
If model metadata is not reliably known, omit the entire model metadata block rather than forwarding a partially guessed providerID/modelID pair.
The minimal canonical session.prompt probe is an experiment for host notice visibility, not the final product notification contract.

The active minimal probe must send only path.id, query.directory, and body.parts text; do not re-add noReply, ignored, agent, variant, model, or direct-shape-first fallback during this probe iteration.
query.directory currently comes from process.cwd(), so host-side meaning may differ from any eventual session/workspace-specific directory semantics.
When manually validating host visibility, start a fresh session if possible so in-memory dedupe does not hide the probe result.
A real-host-visible probe can still have poor UX if the sentence is too terse or omits routing context; treat visibility proof and wording quality as separate acceptance dimensions.
Readable notice copy is still a UX judgment call; document it as reviewer-approved wording improvement, not an objectively final perfect sentence.
Earlier minimal session.prompt probe variants and the later toast-only production variant are historical context only and must be marked superseded where referenced.
Production routing notices must not fall back to toast or chat.message; the active transport is session.prompt with ignored:true payload semantics.
Absolute exclusion from LLM context still depends on host semantics of session.prompt plus ignored:true; document that as a host-side residual risk, not a repo-side fallback gap.
If session.prompt is unavailable, silently skip the routing notice instead of inventing a toast or in-band fallback.
Production notice copy for this path is compact English-only by user override; do not reintroduce Chinese copy unless the user changes direction again.

Keep the critical-role policy set centralized and reviewed over time because gem-orchestrator, gem-planner, gem-debugger, gem-critic, and gem-reviewer are a maintained policy set, not a mechanically derived list.

More aggressive critical-role routing must remain limited boost/hint only: simple may boost to medium, medium stays hint-only, and complex still requires strong signals; do not add per-agent model override even if a specific agent seems to want the top model.

Do not enrich routing notices by guessing provider, environment, or user message content; use only reliable routing payload fields such as agent, tier, source, resolved model, classification reasons, and resolution reason.
The Model line intentionally displays the final resolved model string; do not add redaction unless a new requirement changes the approved decision-information scope.
Use standard npm test for focused routing-hook verification in this repository; npm test -- test/model-routing-hook.test.ts is not compatible with the current test script.

Always scope model-routing hooks to exact canonical Gem Team slugs before any side effect; non-canonical gem-like names, OpenCode built-ins, user agents, and other plugin agents must be skipped without metadata mutation or notices.
If scoped routing imports canonical slugs from a validation module, treat it as currently acceptable but keep future constant extraction as low-risk cleanup rather than a behavior blocker.

failure_modes.scenario: The minimal session.prompt probe is visible in the real host, but the notice text is too terse to explain what happened.
symptoms: User can see the probe
Displayed text looks like `gem-orchestrator · tier route · gpt-5.4`
User still cannot easily tell why this model was chosen
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep the proven session transport, replace the copy with a clearer single-sentence Chinese notice naming the agent, trigger reason, source/tier label, and final model, and avoid expanding scope into toast/chat.message/debug-only routing features.

failure_modes.scenario: PoC cannot verify GitHub load locally.
symptoms: docs/installation-poc.md marks GitHub syntax Blocked
README lacks final install command
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep docs in Pending/Blocked state and do not run optional dist/release claim path.

failure_modes.scenario: Sync conversion changes upstream body content.
symptoms: validate:sync reports non-metadata body diff
hash mismatch without allowlist
failure_modes.mitigation: Preserve body verbatim or add explicit allowlist reason and evidence only for required compatibility metadata/header changes.

failure_modes.scenario: Model-routing hook cannot prove actual mutation without provider call.
symptoms: Only mock/dry-run evidence available
no verified runtime mutation evidence
failure_modes.mitigation: Mark routing capability limited/experimental and do not claim AC-20 as verified.

failure_modes.scenario: Security review finds forbidden capability or secret exposure.
symptoms: provider router/telemetry/slash command appears
env token printed
concrete model ID appears
failure_modes.mitigation: Remove feature/string, rerun validators, repeat T11 before T12.

failure_modes.scenario: Later hook work bypasses the T03 resolver priority chain.
symptoms: agent.model is overwritten
complexity model wins over native agent.model
selected model is ignored when tier model is missing
failure_modes.mitigation: Use resolveModel as the single pure routing contract and keep tests proving native_agent_model priority.

failure_modes.scenario: Unauthenticated public GitHub commit lookup is unavailable during sync.
symptoms: manifest upstreamCommit is unknown-public-github-commit-unavailable
reviewer flags reduced reproducibility
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep the fallback explicit in manifest/review evidence, rerun sync when public commit metadata is available, and never use tokens or env secrets to force resolution.

failure_modes.scenario: Package exports target dist before GitHub install/dist behavior is proven.
symptoms: package exports point to dist
dist is absent or uncommitted
GitHub install/load path cannot resolve package entry
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep GitHub install, package-manager behavior, and dist necessity Pending until verified; only produce or validate dist if the PoC proves it necessary.

failure_modes.scenario: validate:dist allowlist grows beyond defensive validation modules.
symptoms: forbidden strings pass in capability implementation modules
dist scanner no longer reports deterministic path:line reason failures for forbidden config names
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep allowlisting limited to dist/src/routing/resolve-model.js(.d.ts) and dist/src/sync/validation.js(.d.ts); add failing validator tests before changing allowlist scope.

failure_modes.scenario: Plugin instance is rebuilt during the same user workflow.
symptoms: in-memory dedup state is lost
the same default model-selection session notice can appear again after reload/reconstruction
failure_modes.mitigation: Document the behavior as low-risk follow-up compatibility debt; keep dedup scoped to session memory unless a stronger persistence requirement is explicitly approved.

failure_modes.scenario: Host runtime accepts the payload shape but still routes ignored:true prompt content into LLM context.
symptoms: Local tests prove only payload shape
host displays the notice but transcript/context behavior remains uncertain
repository cannot end-to-end prove ignored:true semantics
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep the payload aligned with the current ACP-like contract, document the boundary honestly as host-semantic residual risk, and avoid claiming absolute non-context guarantees from repository tests alone.

failure_modes.scenario: ACP-style metadata is partially unavailable or diverges from the resolved model string.
symptoms: providerID is absent or differs from the displayed resolved model string
variant is missing
notification payload is less informative than ACP on some hosts
failure_modes.mitigation: Forward agent, variant, model.providerID, and model.modelID only on a best-effort basis, keep defaults/dedup/silent-failure boundaries, and document the behavior as parity-seeking rather than visibility-proven.

failure_modes.scenario: Session notice metadata mixes a resolved model from one source with provider metadata from another source.
symptoms: model.modelID reflects resolved agent.model or complexity_models[tier]
model.providerID is copied from input.model.providerID
notification payload implies a provider choice that was never actually resolved
failure_modes.mitigation: Only retain the model metadata block when resolved source is current_selected_model and resolved model exactly matches input.model.id; otherwise omit the entire model block and keep the session-first notification unchanged.

failure_modes.scenario: The minimal canonical session.prompt probe passes local tests but still does not display in the real host UI.
symptoms: Local mock assertions pass
transport shape is canonical path/query/body
real host still shows no visible session notice
failure_modes.mitigation: Document the result as transport-shape and local-mock proof only, keep product claims unfinalized, and verify again in a fresh host session to reduce dedupe interference.

failure_modes.scenario: Probe visibility is misread because dedupe suppresses a repeated notice.
symptoms: Expected notice does not reappear during the same session
Routing decision repeats with identical notice key
Local implementation appears unchanged but host test seems inconsistent
failure_modes.mitigation: Run host verification in a new session or otherwise reset the probe context before concluding the canonical payload shape is invisible.

failure_modes.scenario: Critical-role routing grows inconsistent because the aggressive-upgrade role set drifts over time.
symptoms: One complex coordination/reasoning agent upgrades less aggressively than peers
Reviewer questions why a role is missing from the special set
Behavior changes silently after agent roster evolution
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep the key-role set explicit in routing policy, review it when agent responsibilities change, and preserve the limited boost/hint strategy instead of fixed complex-floor behavior or ad hoc per-agent model overrides.

failure_modes.scenario: Notice wording regresses into terse or non-English-incompatible copy.
symptoms: English notice text is replaced with terse internal fragments
Trigger reason or final model is omitted
Reviewer notes the notice is technically correct but hard to read
failure_modes.mitigation: Preserve the compact English notice contract and cover the copy boundary in routing-hook tests without reintroducing older Chinese production wording as current behavior.

failure_modes.scenario: Production routing notices regress back to toast or chat fallback.
symptoms: client.tui.showToast is reintroduced as active production transport
chat.message is added as a fallback
plan artifacts again describe toast-only as current behavior
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep production transport limited to the ACP-like session.prompt payload, silently skip when unavailable, and treat any toast/chat fallback or stale toast-only documentation as a regression.

failure_modes.scenario: Host session.prompt ignored semantics are not fully non-context even though repo-side payload uses ignored:true.
symptoms: Repo code sends noReply:true plus ignored:true text part
host behavior still determines whether the notice is excluded from LLM context
reviewer can confirm payload shape but not absolute host semantics from code alone
failure_modes.mitigation: Document the remaining risk precisely as host-semantic uncertainty around session.prompt ignored semantics while keeping repo-side behavior free of toast/chat fallbacks.

failure_modes.scenario: Routing notice copy expands beyond reliable decision information and starts guessing context.
symptoms: Notice mentions provider or environment not present in routing payload
Notice summarizes user message content
Reviewer flags inferred metadata
failure_modes.mitigation: Limit notice fields to agent, tier, source, resolved model, classification reasons, and resolution reason; omit anything unavailable instead of guessing.

failure_modes.scenario: Focused npm test invocation is misused as a failure signal.
symptoms: npm test -- test/model-routing-hook.test.ts fails or behaves unexpectedly
Standard npm test still passes
failure_modes.mitigation: Use the repository standard npm test script for validation unless the test script is changed to support file filtering.

failure_modes.scenario: Model-routing hook affects non-plugin agents because ownership scoping happens after preview/config/notice side effects.
symptoms: OpenCode built-in or user-defined agents receive gemTeamModelRouting metadata
Other plugin agents unexpectedly get output.options.model rewritten
Session routing notices appear for agents not managed by this plugin
failure_modes.mitigation: Call isCanonicalGemTeamAgent(input.agent) as the earliest gate and return before config reads, preview calculation, output mutation, metadata writes, or session.prompt notice creation.

decisions.decision: Use 12 waves with one task per wave.
rationale: Eliminates same-wave dependency edges
Improves reviewability and gate ordering

decisions.decision: Make T01 early feasibility PoC mandatory before scaffold-dependent implementation claims.
rationale: OpenCode plugin API was not confirmed at initial planning time; GitHub source syntax is now format-observed only
Docs/dist/release path must not overclaim

decisions.decision: Keep dist optional and PoC-dependent.
rationale: Reviewer/critic rejected default dist submission
GitHub install evidence must determine necessity

decisions.decision: Forbid real provider calls in default PoC and tests.
rationale: Avoid cost/side effects
Avoid exposing API keys/tokens
Plan can use dry-run/mock/local OpenCode load evidence

decisions.decision: Use PluginModule default export as the primary scaffold shape after T01.
rationale: Installed SDK exposes PluginModule
Fixture typecheck passed
Shape matches PRD section 5.7

decisions.decision: Keep GitHub install/load and dist necessity Pending after syntax-only follow-up evidence.
rationale: Official docs found in T01 document local and npm plugin paths
Follow-up evidence observed only redacted github:<owner>/<repo>#<ref> config syntax
Package export behavior depends on unverified GitHub install mechanics

decisions.decision: Use node:test plus built TypeScript output for the scaffold test gate.
rationale: Meets minimal dependency goal
Validates exported plugin shape without provider calls
Avoids introducing an extra test runner before feature work

decisions.decision: Keep sync and validation scripts as placeholders until later waves.
rationale: T02 scope is scaffold only
Later waves own sync and generated-asset behavior
Keeps the initial package buildable and typecheckable

decisions.decision: Missing or unknown complexity tiers fallback to medium in T03 pure routing.
rationale: Medium is the safest neutral tier across simple/complex uncertainty
Fallback is deterministic and reason-tagged for tests and later hook metadata

decisions.decision: Keep T03 model values opaque strings and avoid model ID validation inside resolveModel.
rationale: PRD forbids hardcoded concrete model IDs but user-provided model values are OpenCode-owned opaque strings
Security validation belongs at repository artifact scanning boundaries

decisions.decision: Reasoning-critical roles affect tier classification only, not model selection dimensions.
rationale: PRD AC-11/AC-12 forbid role_models and per-agent overrides
This preserves three-tier global routing while allowing safe tier hints

decisions.decision: Store synchronized upstream bodies in agents/generated and manifest metadata in agents/generated/manifest.json for T04.
rationale: Keeps generated artifacts stable and inspectable
Later T07 config injection can import or read one stable manifest path
Avoids rewriting upstream behavior while still recording audit metadata

decisions.decision: Use unauthenticated GitHub commits API only for best-effort commit metadata and raw.githubusercontent.com GET requests for agent bodies.
rationale: Satisfies read-only/no-token sync guard
Allows validation to proceed with explicit unknown value if public commit lookup is unavailable
Avoids reading or printing environment secrets

decisions.decision: Treat Wave 4/T04 as complete and continue to Wave 5.
rationale: Generated 16 upstream agent bodies and schemaVersion 1 manifest
Required sync/typecheck/build/test/model-id validations passed
Reviewer found no blocking issues

decisions.decision: Use a runtime filesystem loader instead of importing JSON/Markdown directly.
rationale: Current tsconfig does not include JSON or Markdown module imports
Filesystem loading works from source and dist paths
Hash parity checks keep generated prompt bodies preserved

decisions.decision: For existing OpenCode agent config, user fields override generated defaults.
rationale: Preserves user model, prompt, permission, mode, and unknown fields
Avoids destructive prompt overwrite while still filling missing generated fields

decisions.decision: Complete config hook injection in Wave 5/T05 while leaving Wave 6 and later validation/routing/docs gates pending.
rationale: Runtime loader and injection were implemented together using the generated manifest and bodies
Plan naming drift is recorded without inventing new tasks
GitHub install, dist/package-manager behavior, and actual request model mutation remain pending

decisions.decision: Keep manifest metadata separate from OpenCode agent config.
rationale: OpenCode agent config should receive only supported fields
Manifest remains the audit source for hashes, routingTargets, source URLs, commits, and sync metadata

decisions.decision: Treat Wave 6/T06 as complete and continue to Wave 7 while keeping AC-20 partial.
rationale: previewModelRouting and chat.params mock/type evidence passed all local guards
Reviewer found no blocking issues
Actual OpenCode request model mutation remains unverified without provider/API calls

decisions.decision: Expose package main and exports[./server] to dist/src/index.js and gate it with validate:dist.
rationale: OpenCode package target inspection expects exports[./server] or package main for server plugins
The first local package-path install failed without a server target
The second local package-path install passed after adding server entrypoints and built dist

decisions.decision: Document GitHub source install syntax as format observed / end-to-end Pending and provide only local/path development install guidance.
rationale: Official OpenCode plugin docs verified local and npm paths but not a final GitHub source syntax
Follow-up read-only global config inspection observed only a redacted plugin array format
No external publication or global config writes were used
Avoids overclaiming PRD AC-16

decisions.decision: Treat Wave 7/T07 as complete and continue to the final waves with pending remote-source risks preserved.
rationale: Local package-path install and 16-agent visibility were verified in a temp fixture
Reviewer found no blocking issues
GitHub source syntax format is observed only; GitHub package-manager behavior, remote dist necessity, AC-20 runtime mutation, and package exports-to-dist risk remain explicitly pending

decisions.decision: Treat final validation hardening as complete and ready for final review, not commit-ready without reviewer/orchestrator git inspection.
rationale: Full validation chain passed after broad dist forbidden-surface scanning was added
Pending GitHub/runtime risks remain accurately labeled
All implementation artifacts remain uncommitted and require final status/diff/log review before any commit

decisions.decision: Default model-selection notification should use toast and be enabled by default without debug_model_routing.
rationale: User explicitly chose default-visible notification behavior
OpenCode SDK exposes client.tui.showToast() and opencode-acp provides the reference direction
chat.message and session.prompt are intentionally excluded to avoid claiming a non-context notification path they do not faithfully provide

decisions.decision: Fallback behavior when toast is unavailable is silent skip only.
rationale: Maintains low-risk compatibility across SDK surfaces
Avoids misrepresenting ignored session prompts or chat messages as equivalent to a real toast
Keeps the wrapper minimal and non-invasive

decisions.decision: Supersede the earlier toast-first notification direction with a session-first notification direction.
rationale: User explicitly replaced the earlier TUI toast decision with a session-based approach
The active behavior must still show model selection by default while removing any dependency on debug_model_routing
Historical toast conclusions should remain documented but clearly marked as superseded rather than deleted

decisions.decision: Prefer ACP-like client.session.prompt(...) with noReply: true and an ignored text part, with compatibility fallback across prompt call shapes.
rationale: Matches the new user-selected session transport direction
Host support may vary between direct-shape and SDK path/body-shape prompt invocation
Compatibility fallback preserves behavior without overclaiming universal host support

decisions.decision: Keep model-selection notification default-enabled with in-session deduplication and silent failure boundaries.
rationale: Default visibility remains a user requirement
Memory-only dedup reduces repeated-session spam without adding persistence complexity
Silent failure avoids breaking chat flow when the host rejects or lacks the session prompt transport

decisions.decision: Align the session notification payload more closely with opencode-acp by forwarding ACP-style metadata when available, without changing the session-first transport strategy.
rationale: User explicitly requested an opencode-acp comparison and trial implementation
Follow-up analysis showed the gap was not only ignored: true but also missing agent, variant, model.providerID, and model.modelID metadata
Best-effort forwarding improves parity while keeping honest boundaries around unverified real-host visibility

decisions.decision: Do not guess provider metadata for session notices; if model provenance is not fully reliable, omit the entire model metadata block.
rationale: Reviewer identified that pairing resolved model.modelID with input.model.providerID mixes sources and guesses provider identity on agent.model and complexity_models[tier] paths
Reliable metadata exists only when the resolved source is current_selected_model and the resolved model exactly matches input.model.id
Omission preserves honest ACP-style parity boundaries without changing routing, transport, or notification defaults

decisions.decision: Switch the active notice transport experiment to the minimal canonical session.prompt path/query/body shape.
rationale: The current goal is to test whether the host will display the smallest canonical session notice shape, not to finalize the richer product payload
Reducing the payload to path.id, query.directory, and body.parts text isolates transport-shape behavior from optional metadata and flags
Reviewer approved the change as a probe-only iteration

decisions.decision: Do not send noReply, ignored, agent, variant, model, or direct-shape-first fallback in the minimal canonical probe.
rationale: These fields and fallback behaviors are intentionally removed to keep the probe narrowly focused on the canonical path/query/body transport shape
The probe should not mix host-visibility testing with ACP-style metadata parity concerns
Reviewer-approved scope explicitly excludes reverting to toast/chat.message and excludes debug_model_routing

decisions.decision: Keep routing priority and output.options.model logic unchanged while probing notice visibility.
rationale: The probe is about notice transport shape, not model routing semantics
Preserving OpenCode agent.model > plugin complexity_models[tier] > current selected model avoids accidental product-scope drift
Reviewer explicitly approved no routing-priority change

decisions.decision: Superseded: an intermediate iteration made key complex agents more aggressive by raising their minimum tier to complex instead of overriding their models directly.
rationale: Satisfies the user request for higher-end model preference on complex agents while preserving the routing contract
Avoids adding per-agent model override, which would violate the constrained routing design
Superseded by the later user clarification that more aggressive should not mean always using the highest tier

decisions.decision: Use a readable Chinese single-sentence routing notice instead of terse internal fragments.
rationale: The minimal probe was visible but the old text was hard to read and incomplete
The user needs the notice to explain who triggered routing, why it happened, what tier/source label applied, and which final model won

decisions.decision: Supersede the toast-only production routing notice with an ACP-like client.session.prompt(...) payload that uses noReply: true and ignored:true text parts.
rationale: User explicitly replaced the toast-only direction and asked not to use client.tui.showToast
The implementation now matches the current reviewer-approved payload shape
This keeps the notice visible while relying on host ignored semantics rather than toast transport

decisions.decision: Use payload { path: { id }, query: { directory }, body: { noReply: true, parts: [{ type: 'text', text, ignored: true }] } } with no toast/chat fallback.
rationale: The user requested an ACP-like session prompt that should remain out of LLM context
The repository implementation and tests validate this exact payload shape
Silent skip preserves UX constraints better than reintroducing toast or chat fallback

decisions.decision: Keep compact English notice copy, session-memory dedup, limited critical-role boost/hint behavior, and the existing routing priority while forbidding new routing dimensions.
rationale: The English notice copy was explicitly preserved
The user confirmed dedupe and routing invariants should remain
Reviewer-approved scope excludes debug_model_routing, role_models, agent_complexity_models, and per-agent model IDs
Later correction replaced the fixed complex-floor wording with simple->medium boost, medium hint-only, and complex-only-from-strong-signals behavior

decisions.decision: Expand production routing notice copy to English-only multiline decision information.
rationale: User requested messages to include decision-useful information
Multiline structure improves readability over terse routing fragments
The change preserves the ACP-like hidden-context session.prompt transport and existing routing priority

decisions.decision: Use only reliable routing payload fields for notice decision information.
rationale: Avoids guessing provider, environment, or user message content
Keeps the notice auditable and reviewer-approved
Preserves security and privacy boundaries while still explaining the routing decision

decisions.decision: Scope model routing to this plugin's exact canonical 16 Gem Team agent slugs only.
rationale: User explicitly required the plugin to route only agents it adds and not affect other agents
Exact canonical slug matching prevents accidental mutation of OpenCode built-ins, user agents, other plugin agents, and non-canonical gem-like agents
Early return before config/preview/output/notice side effects preserves unrelated agent behavior while leaving canonical gem routing unchanged

conventions: Use docs/PRD.md as product source of truth.
Artifacts and IDs use concise English identifiers.
Package code should be TypeScript ESM unless T01 proves a different plugin shape is required.
Validators fail non-zero with actionable messages.
Documentation separates verified facts from pending or blocked PoC items.
Use docs/PRD.md, not docs/PRD.yaml, as the PRD source in this repository.
Manifest entries use schemaVersion 1 fields slug, localSlug, name, sourceUrl, rawUrl, upstreamCommit, sourceBodySha256, bodyPath, bodyBytes, syncBatchId, syncedAt, and metadata.
Config hook injection must be non-destructive: generated defaults first, existing user config wins.
Newly injected generated agents omit model; only pre-existing OpenCode user config may carry model.
Model-routing documentation and review notes must label chat.params request mutation as Partial/Pending unless runtime evidence proves AC-20 without provider/API calls.
Before any commit, inspect git status, git diff, and git log --oneline -10; if dist is committed, ensure clean regeneration and validate:dist green.
validate:dist allowlists defensive validation/rejection strings only in dist/src/routing/resolve-model.js(.d.ts) and dist/src/sync/validation.js(.d.ts).
Superseded historical notification decisions should stay documented, but the currently effective model-selection notification path must be identified explicitly.

Current model-selection notification documentation must describe the exact ACP-like payload { path: { id }, query: { directory }, body: { noReply: true, parts: [{ type: 'text', text, ignored: true }] } }, in-session memory deduplication, and silent failure when the host does not support or rejects session.prompt.

ACP-style session metadata fields agent, variant, model.providerID, and model.modelID should be documented as best-effort passthrough only when truly available, not as guaranteed or runtime-visibility-proven fields.
Do not guess providerID for session notices; if the full model metadata cannot be proven, omit the entire model block.
Current probe documentation must distinguish probe-only transport experiments from final product decisions.
Minimal-probe and toast-only notice narratives must be marked superseded when referenced from current plan context.
Document that query.directory currently comes from process.cwd() and that real host visibility remains unverified even when local mock validation passes.

Current production notice documentation must identify client.session.prompt(...) with ignored:true payload semantics as the only active transport and must explicitly state that toast/chat production fallbacks are absent.

When documenting transcript-safety, distinguish repo-side guarantees from host-semantic guarantees: the repo sends only the session.prompt hidden-context payload, but absolute exclusion from LLM context still depends on host ignored semantics.
Production routing notice copy is compact English-only; older Chinese/session-notice guidance and toast-only guidance are historical context, not the current production contract.

Production routing notices should present decision-useful information as English-only multiline text using reliable routing payload fields only: agent, tier, source, resolved model, classification reasons, and resolution reason.
Do not document provider/env/user-message details in routing notices unless those fields are explicitly and reliably supplied by the routing payload.
Model-routing side effects must be owned-scope first: exact canonical Gem Team slug match before config reads, preview computation, output mutation, metadata writes, or session notice emission.

scoped_model_routing_followup.user_requirement: Only route agents added/managed by this plugin; do not affect other intelligent agents.

scope_contract.included_agents: Exact canonical 16 gem-* slugs from CANONICAL_GEM_TEAM_SLUGS.
scope_contract.matching_rule: input.agent must exactly match a canonical Gem Team slug.

implementation_summary: applyChatParamsModelRouting gates on isCanonicalGemTeamAgent(input.agent) before reading activeConfig or computing previewModelRouting.
